entries.title: What it is and where to look
entries.body:
The PHP functions extract($_GET/$_POST), import_request_variables(), parse_str() and register_globals import user input into the current variables.
extract() defaults to EXTR_OVERWRITE -> it OVERWRITES existing variables with the same name.
Impact: bypassing logic/authentication, a gadget for LFI and RCE. Look in the source for extract($_GET) and the like.

entries.title: Vulnerable code - authentication bypass
entries.body:
<?php
    $authenticated = false;
    extract($_GET);
    if ($authenticated) {
        echo "Access granted!";
    } else {
        echo "Access denied!";
    }
?>

entries.title: Vulnerable code - controlling the include path
Overwriting $GLOBALS
entries.body:
In the code: extract($_GET);

Attack:
http://example.com/vuln.php?GLOBALS[admin]=1

Note: as of PHP 8.1.0, writing to the entire $GLOBALS array is no longer supported.

entries.title: Protection - EXTR_SKIP
entries.body: extract($_GET, EXTR_SKIP); // do not overwrite existing variables
